bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just begun (t=0.0). The opponent offered their assumed highest utility bid which provides exactly 0.2 utility for me. Since we are at the start of the negotiation, I will counter with my absolute best option (yielding a utility of 1.0) to set a strong baseline.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.02). The opponent has made a very small concession on issueA, but their offer remains far below an acceptable utility for me. To show reciprocal flexibility while protecting my highest priority issues (issueA and issueB), I will make a minor concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, remaining very close to my maximum possible utility.

The time fraction is very early (0.04) in the negotiation. The opponent is making marginal concessions on issueA and issueC, showing some flexibility. Because issueA and issueB make up the vast majority of my utility (0.7 combined), I will hold absolutely firm on both. I have already conceded on issueD, so I will now make a slight concession on issueC to signal my continued willingness to engage while preserving a highly favorable total utility.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06), and the opponent has just repeated their previous offer, showing resistance to concede further for now. I will make a small concession on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, in order to maintain momentum without sacrificing much utility, while holding firm on my highest-priority issues, issueA and issueB.

We are still very early in the negotiation at t=0.08. The opponent has held fast on issueD, offering valueD in every single turn, which suggests they value it highly. Since my importance on issueD is the lowest (0.1), I will concede issueD to valueD to show cooperative intent, while holding firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB) as my priority is to maximize my overall utility.

In this early stage (t=0.1), my goal is to continue probing the opponent's preferences while maintaining a high utility (>0.8). Since the opponent seems entrenched on issueD=valueD and has been adjusting issueB and issueA, I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue (issueA) and the previously established issueD=valueD. By conceding slightly on issueB to valueB and bringing issueC back to valueA, I offer a new combination to see if they value concessions on issueB more than those on issueC.

In the early phase of this negotiation (t=0.12), I will maintain a high target utility (above 0.8). My opponent is starting to concede on my most important issue, issueA, reaching valueB in their last offer, but they are persistently proposing valueD for my lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD). I will hold firm on my highest priorities (valueA for issueA and issueB) while giving them valueC for issueC and valueD for issueD to concede incrementally towards their preferred values.

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.14). The opponent has made a small concession on my most valuable issue (issueA to valueB), showing movement. To encourage this while maintaining high utility for myself, I will hold firm on issueA (valueA) and offer a slight concession on the remaining issues (issueB, issueC to valueB and issueD to valueC) yielding a utility of 0.812 for myself.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.16), leaving ample time to explore mutual gains. I see from the opponent's recent bids that they have shown willingness to concede mildly on issueA, issueB, and issueC. Since issueA is my highest priority (weight 0.4), I will maintain my top value here, but I am willing to meet the opponent on issueD since it's my least important issue (weight 0.1), while keeping issueB and issueC at slightly compromised levels to maintain my utility around 0.78.

The opponent has made significant concessions on my highest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB) by offering valueB for both, demonstrating a willingness to cooperate. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I want to maintain a high utility (>0.7) while showing flexibility. I will hold firm on issueA at valueA, concede issueB to valueB, match their previous offer of valueC for issueC, and fully accommodate their strong preference for valueD on my lowest-weighted issueD.

The current time fraction is 0.2, leaving plenty of room to explore tradeoffs. The opponent is firmly asserting `valueD` on issueC and issueD, while conceding to `valueB` on my highest-priority issues, issueA and issueB. I will accept their preference for `valueD` on issueC, but try to compensate by asking for a slight concession on issueD while standing firm on my most important issue (issueA).

The negotiation is still relatively early (t=0.22), and I need to start finding acceptable trade-offs without making drastic concessions. The opponent has continuously pushed for 'valueD' on issues C and D, while showing more flexibility on issues A and B (moving from valueD to valueB). Since issues A and B are my most important issues, I will hold firm on 'valueA' for both, while fully conceding on their apparent priority issues by proposing 'valueD' for C and D. This yields a strong utility of 0.760 for me while directly addressing their strongest preferences.

The opponent has made highly significant concessions, offering me my top value for issueB and my second-best for issueA. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I am aiming for a slightly higher utility than the opponent's latest offer. Based on their past bids, they seem willing to accept valueC for issueD and valueD for issueC, so I will incorporate those into my offer while pushing to regain my top choice for issueA.

The opponent has recently shown a willingness to offer `valueA` on `issueB`, which is my top preference and holds a high weight (0.3). Meanwhile, they consistently target `valueB` on `issueA` and `valueD` on `issueC` and `issueD`. Because we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold firm on getting my maximum utility on `issueA` and `issueB`, while beginning to concede on `issueC` to align with their preference (`valueD`). I will demand my top value for `issueD` to maintain high overall utility, giving the opponent a new combination to evaluate.

The opponent has made highly favorable concessions on `issueB` (offering `valueA`), but remains steadfast on `issueC` and `issueD` at `valueD`. Since `issueA` and `issueB` are my most heavily weighted priorities (combined weight of 0.7), I will gladly accept their offer on `issueB` and insist on my preference for `issueA`. In exchange, I will concede `issueD` to their preferred `valueD` and offer a slight compromise on `issueC` to keep the utility above 0.8, seeing if they'll yield there instead.

The opponent has made significant concessions by offering my preferred value (valueA) on issueB, which is my second-most important issue. Since we are still in the relatively early phase (t=0.3), I want to lock in valueA for issues A and B while showing flexibility on the less critical issues (C and D) to find a middle ground. I will offer valueD for issueC (which the opponent seems to strongly prefer) and valueB for issueD, yielding a solid utility of 0.813.

The opponent has repeatedly requested `issueA: valueB`, making it clear that this issue is of high importance to them. By conceding to `valueB` on `issueA` (which still holds decent utility for me), I can maintain my top preferences for the remaining issues (`issueB`, `issueC`, and `issueD`), targeting a high utility of roughly 0.89. This trade-off tests whether they are willing to accept my most preferred values on the other fronts in exchange for their preferred outcome on the most contentious issue.

The opponent was consistently offering {"issueA": "valueB", "issueB": "valueA", "issueC": "valueD", "issueD": "valueD"} between t=0.24 and t=0.30, indicating a willingness to compromise on issueB in exchange for valueD on issues C and D. However, when I requested "valueA" across the board on issues C and D, they retracted their concession on issueB. To steer the negotiation back toward mutual concessions, I will offer their preferred "valueB" on issueA and "valueD" on issueD, while maintaining "valueA" on issueB and aiming for a moderate compromise ("valueB") on issueC, landing at a respectable utility of 0.758 for me.

The current time is early-mid negotiation (t=0.36). The opponent previously conceded to offering `valueA` on `issueB` (my second most important issue) while requesting `valueB` on `issueA` and `valueD` on `issueC` and `issueD`. My goal is to draw them back towards `issueB: valueA` without giving up too much ground on my top priority `issueA`, so I will propose an offer holding `issueA` and `issueB` at their maximum utilities while continuing to show flexibility on the less critical `issueC` and `issueD`.

The opponent had previously offered `issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD` before retreating their concession on `issueB`. My utility is concentrated on `issueA` and `issueB`, so I want to guide them back to offering high values for these two issues. I am willing to concede on my lower-priority issues (`issueC` and `issueD`), so I will offer a compromise that asks for their earlier generous `issueB` value while conceding on `issueD` entirely and moving towards their preference on `issueC`.

The opponent previously offered `issueA: valueB` and `issueB: valueA`, yielding 0.65+ utility, but has since retreated to a much less favorable bid. To encourage them to compromise, I will pivot back towards `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueB`, conceding on the lower-weight issues `issueC` and `issueD` to find an acceptable overlap. At this mid-game stage (t=0.40), I am targeting a utility of around 0.76 to maintain pressure while opening up alternatives.

The current time is 0.42, so we are in the mid-game phase. The opponent has repeatedly signaled an interest in valueB for issueA, and has also offered valueA and valueB for issueB. However, they are demanding valueD for issues C and D, which provide me very little utility. I will accept their proposal for valueB on issueA and valueA on issueB, and attempt to compromise by proposing moderate valueC for issueC and valueB for issueD, maintaining a solid utility around 0.76 for myself.

The opponent has repeatedly shown a strong preference for `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`, but has importantly shown willingness to concede on my most critical issues, proposing `issueA: valueB` and `issueB: valueA` previously. At t=0.44, I will capitalize on their flexibility for the first two issues by proposing `issueA: valueB` and `issueB: valueA`, while making a small concession on `issueC` and `issueD` to `valueB` and `valueC` to test the waters and seek a high utility compromise above 0.75.

The opponent has retreated slightly from a past offer of `issueB: valueA` to `issueB: valueC`, while remaining fixated on `issueA: valueB`, `issueC: valueD`, and `issueD: valueD`. Since we are at t=0.46, I will continue to signal flexibility across different issues rather than fully ceding to their preferred structure. I will offer a combination that gives them their preferred `issueA` and `issueD` values while attempting to secure high utility for myself on `issueB` and `issueC`.

The negotiation is approaching the halfway mark (t=0.48), and the opponent has previously shown willingness to offer 'valueA' for 'issueB' as long as they get their preferred values on other issues. Since 'issueC' and 'issueD' are of lower importance to me, I am setting both of them to the opponent's consistently requested 'valueD'. I will hold firm on 'issueA' and 'issueB' at 'valueA' to maintain a high utility (0.76) for myself.

The opponent has shown a strong preference for `valueD` on `issueC` and `issueD`, while occasionally offering `valueA` or `valueB` on `issueB`. Since it is mid-game (t=0.50), I will target a utility around 0.81 to leave some room for negotiation. I will compromise slightly on `issueC` and `issueD` to `valueB` and `valueC` while holding my top values on `issueA` and `issueB`.

The opponent is consistently holding firm on `issueD: valueD` and strongly favors `valueD` for `issueC`, although they occasionally offered `valueC`. They have showed flexibility early on `issueB` (offering `valueA`) and `issueA` (`valueB`). Because we are mid-way at `t=0.52`, I'll concede on their heavily weighted issues (`issueD: valueD`) while expecting them to concede on my highly valued combinations. I will propose a moderate compromise yielding around 0.73 utility, balancing past concessions by accepting `valueB` for `issueB` and `valueD` for `issueD`, while holding firm on `issueA`.

The opponent has repeatedly insisted on `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`, while fluctuating on `issueB` (recently settling on `valueB`). At t=0.54, there is still time, but I need to signal a willingness to find a middle ground without sacrificing too much utility on my most heavily weighted issue (`issueA`, weight 0.4). I will offer a compromise that fully grants their apparent preference on `issueC` and `issueB`, maintains my top choice on `issueA`, and seeks a modest concession on `issueD`.

The negotiation has passed the halfway mark (t=0.56). The opponent consistently insists on valueD for issueC and issueD, which are my lower-weight issues. In return, I need strong values in issueA and issueB to maintain utility. I will propose an offer that gives the opponent their preferred valueB for issueA, asks for valueA on issueB, and tries to find a compromise on issueC and issueD to test their flexibility while keeping my utility above 0.75.

The opponent is consistently holding firm on issueC: valueD and issueD: valueD, while showing flexibility on issueB (having previously offered valueA, valueB, and valueC). I want to maintain high utility on my top two priorities (issueA and issueB). Since we are nearing the middle-to-late part of the negotiation, I will offer a compromise that moves closer to their preferences on issueC (offering valueC) while keeping my targeted preferences for issueA and issueB, attempting to uncover if they prioritize issueC or issueD more.

The negotiation has reached t=0.6, so it's time to start finding a workable compromise. The opponent has repeatedly signaled strong preferences for `issueA: valueB`, `issueC: valueD`, and `issueD: valueD`, while historically showing flexibility to concede on `issueB` (even offering `valueA` at t=0.24 to t=0.30). I will incorporate their desired values for `issueA` and `issueC` while holding firm on `issueB: valueA` to protect my core utility, and propose `issueD: valueB` as a minor compromise.

The opponent previously demonstrated a willingness to accept `valueB` for `issueA` and `valueA` for `issueB` (between t=0.24 and t=0.30). Because my utility weight for `issueA` is slightly higher, I will meet them at their preferred `valueB` for `issueA` but insist on `valueA` for `issueB`. I will offer moderate concessions on `issueC` and `issueD` to propose a balanced option aiming to keep my utility around 0.76 as we enter the later stages of the negotiation.

The current time is 0.64, moving towards the late phase of the negotiation. The opponent's offers indicate a strong preference for issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD, and issueA: valueB, while previously showing flexibility by offering issueB: valueA. I will concede issueA to valueB and issueD to valueD, maintaining my high-utility preference for issueB: valueA and proposing issueC: valueB to secure a utility of approximately 0.76.

The opponent is consistently holding firm on `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`, but has recently conceded to `issueB: valueA` and `issueA: valueB`. This gives me a decent utility (~0.65), but since we are at t=0.66, I have some time to try negotiating a slightly better deal. I will accept their generous offers on `issueA` and `issueB`, but I will propose moderate concessions on `issueC` and `issueD` to see if they are flexible on those issues before I fully concede to their terms.

The opponent is consistently offering valueD for both issueC and issueD, indicating these issues may have high importance to them. Meanwhile, they have conceded on issueB to provide valueA, bringing their offer's utility to 0.652. Since my weights for issueC (0.2) and issueD (0.1) are lower than issueA (0.4) and issueB (0.3), I will offer a compromise that fully concedes issueC to their preferred valueD, but asks for my top preferred values on issueA and issueB, preserving a high utility of 0.787 for myself while we are still at t=0.68.

The opponent has repeatedly signaled strong preferences for `valueD` on `issueC` and `valueD` on `issueD`, while demonstrating flexibility on `issueB` (recently offering `valueA`). In order to drive the negotiation towards an agreement by the deadline while protecting my critical utility, I am matching their preferred values on `issueD` and partially conceding on `issueC`, while securing my top value on `issueB` and a high-utility value on `issueA`. This represents a balanced compromise at t=0.70, testing whether a slight shift on `issueC` alongside their `issueB` concession will result in acceptance.

The opponent has made substantial concessions on issueB (now offering valueA) while remaining absolutely inflexible on issueC and issueD (valueD). Since we are entering the late-mid game (t=0.72), I will concede entirely to their preference on issueC and issueD. In exchange, I will request valueA for both issueA and issueB, exploring if they are willing to yield on issueA now that I am giving them their exact demands on two other issues, which keeps my target utility at a strong 0.76.

The opponent is consistently holding firm on `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`, but has conceded `issueB: valueA` multiple times, which is highly beneficial (weight 0.3, max utility) for me. Although their last offer yields a respectable 0.652 utility, the deadline is not immediately pressing. I will temporarily meet their core demands on issues A and C but try adjusting issue D to find middle ground before eventually accepting their firm offer if necessary.

The opponent has offered `issueA: valueA`, `issueB: valueB`, `issueC: valueD`, `issueD: valueD`, which gives me a utility of 0.679. Considering they are completely inflexible on `issueC` and `issueD`, they likely have high weights for those issues. However, they have shown flexibility between `valueA` and `valueB` for `issueA` and `issueB`. I will counter by insisting on `valueA` for both `issueA` and `issueB`, while keeping their preferred values for the other two issues to signal compromise where it matters to them.

The opponent has shown a strong preference for `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`. Recently, they have conceded on `issueA` to `valueA`, while holding `issueB` at `valueB`. To maximize my utility while finding a mutually acceptable agreement as the deadline approaches, I will hold firm on `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueA` while conceding on `issueC` to their preferred `valueD`, asking for only a minor concession on `issueD` to `valueC` which is my lowest-weight issue. This balances our priorities and aims towards a high-utility compromise.

The opponent has shown flexibility only on issueA and issueB, recently switching to offering 'valueA' for issueA and 'valueB' for issueB, while rigidly sticking to 'valueD' for issueC and issueD. Since issueA is my highest value issue, their recent offers yield a solid utility of approximately 0.68. I will accept their preferred values for A and B but request a small concession on issueC to see if they are willing to flex slightly before I yield to their exact offer near the deadline.

The opponent has shown significant resistance on issues C and D, insisting on valueD for both since early in the negotiation. While I attempted to get valueC on issue C, the opponent's latest offer retreated to valueC on issue B, indicating frustration or a negotiation tactic. To secure an agreement and prevent further regression as the deadline approaches, I will concede to their previously sustained offer of valueB on issue B and valueD on issues C and D, locking in my top preference on issue A.

I noticed the opponent recently rejected my acceptance of their prior offer, suggesting their acceptance threshold is tightening or cycling. Their most consistently generous past offer to me was `issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD` (yielding me a utility of 0.652). I will propose this exact offer, as it provides a better return than their current offer and they have demonstrated a willingness to accept it in the past.

The current time is 0.86, placing us late in the negotiation. The opponent recently offered {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD} multiple times between t=0.76 and t=0.80, which provides me with a reasonable utility of ~0.68. I will propose this exact offer again to close the deal, as the opponent has already demonstrated willingness to accept these terms.

The opponent has recently demanded an offer that yields a utility of 0.601 for me (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD). Given that we are at t=0.88 and the deadline is closely approaching, I must prepare to find a middle ground without dropping strictly right to 0.601. I will attempt to secure issueA: valueA and issueB: valueB (which the opponent previously proposed) while offering a slight concession on issueD to seek an acceptable compromise.

The negotiation is in the late stages (t=0.90). The opponent has been consistently insisting on valueD for both issueC and issueD, for which I have low importance. However, they previously offered {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB} alongside those values, which gives me a relatively high utility of 0.679. I will offer this exact combination to secure a mutually beneficial agreement before the deadline.

The negotiations are approaching the deadline. The opponent has consistently demonstrated a strict requirement for `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD` across almost all their proposals. Earlier (between t=0.76 and t=0.80), the opponent was willing to offer `issueA: valueA` and `issueB: valueB`, which yields a very high utility for me, but due to crossing bids we missed an agreement. I will propose this favorable intersection again to finalize the deal before time runs out.

The deadline is imminent at t=0.94. The opponent has firmly settled on valueD for both issueC and issueD, which are my lower priority issues, so I will concede on those to secure agreement. For the higher priority issues, they have historically offered both {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB} and {issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA}; since my last offer of the former was rejected, I will now offer the latter, which still yields a favorable utility of 0.652.
